name: Via Verde Huesca-Alerre & Rio Isuela — gravel return from Huesca
name_original: Vía Verde Huesca-Alerre & Río Isuela — vuelta gravel desde Huesca
region_intro: Popular cycling routes in Hoya de Huesca traverse a diverse landscape, transitioning from the rugged Pre-Pyrenean mountains to expansive agricultural plains. The region is characterized by striking geological formations, including the towering Mallos de Riglos, and river-carved canyons. Touring cyclists can explore varied terrain, from mountainous areas to gentler, rolling hills and steppes. Best touring cycling routes around Hoya De Huesca The most popular touring cycling route is Montearagón Castle and Saso de Montearagón Loop, a 15.8 miles (25.5 km) trail that takes 1 hour 48 minutes to complete, offering views of historical sites. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Mallos de Riglos & Mirador de los Buitres — return via the Sierra de Loarre, a difficult 25.5 miles (41.0 km) path. This route features dramatic rock formations and opportunities to observe birds of prey. Local touring cyclists also love the Via Verde Huesca-Alerre & Rio Isuela — gravel return from Huesca, a 10.5 miles (17.0 km) trail leading through agricultural plains and along the Rio Isuela, often completed in about 1 hour 13 minutes. Touring cycling around Hoya de Huesca is defined by the impressive Mallos de Riglos, the foothills of the Pre-Pyrenean Sierras, and serene reservoirs. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy gravel paths to more challenging mountain trails. The routes in Hoya de Huesca are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 90 reviews. More than 1500 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Hoya de Huesca's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available in Hoya de Huesca?
A: Hoya de Huesca offers a wide selection of touring cycling routes, with over 190 options available. These routes traverse diverse landscapes, from the Pre-Pyrenean mountains to expansive agricultural plains, catering to various preferences and skill levels.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes in Hoya de Huesca?
A: The region's terrain is incredibly varied. You'll find everything from easy gravel paths around serene reservoirs like La Sotonera to more challenging mountain trails in the Pre-Pyrenean Sierras. Expect to encounter dramatic geological formations such as the Mallos de Riglos, river-carved canyons, and rolling agricultural plains.

Q: Are there touring cycling routes suitable for beginners or families in Hoya de Huesca?
A: Yes, Hoya de Huesca has options for less experienced cyclists and families. There are over 30 easy touring cycling routes. A good example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e974886923/via-verde-huesca-alerre-rio-isuela-gravel-return-from-huesca" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Via Verde Huesca-Alerre & Rio Isuela — gravel return from Huesca&lt;/a&gt;, which offers a gentler ride through agricultural plains.

Q: What are some notable natural landmarks or viewpoints I can see while cycling?
A: The region is rich in natural beauty. You can cycle past the impressive Mallos de Riglos, towering conglomerate rock formations, and potentially spot birds of prey like griffon vultures. The Sierra y Cañones de Guara Nature Reserve also features stunning ravines and gorges. For panoramic views, consider visiting &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/349408" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Camino del Cielo - Panoramic View&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1087321" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico del Águila&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes in Hoya de Huesca?
A: Yes, many routes are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e980015875/alto-gallego-circular-from-the-embalse-de-pena" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto Gállego — circular from the Embalse de Peña&lt;/a&gt; offers a scenic circular tour. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e979471478/sotonera-reservoir-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sotonera Reservoir Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which provides a pleasant ride around the water.

Q: What historical sites or castles can I explore along the cycling routes?
A: Hoya de Huesca boasts a rich history. You can cycle near impressive Romanesque architecture, such as the well-preserved &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3983714" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Loarre Castle&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e974918905/montearagon-castle-and-saso-de-montearagon-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Montearagón Castle and Saso de Montearagón Loop&lt;/a&gt; specifically takes you past the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5254839" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Montearagón Castle&lt;/a&gt;. &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/793375" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Marcuello Castle&lt;/a&gt; also offers spectacular views over La Galliguera and the Mallos.

Q: What is the best time of year to go touring cycling in Hoya de Huesca?
A: The diverse climate of Hoya de Huesca means cycling is enjoyable for much of the year. Spring and autumn generally offer pleasant temperatures for touring. Summers can be hot, especially in the plains, while mountainous areas might be cooler. Always check local weather forecasts before heading out.

Q: Are there any challenging touring cycling routes for experienced cyclists?
A: Absolutely. For experienced cyclists seeking a challenge, Hoya de Huesca offers over 70 difficult routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e975143941/mallos-de-riglos-mirador-de-los-buitres-return-via-the-sierra-de-loarre" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mallos de Riglos & Mirador de los Buitres — return via the Sierra de Loarre&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult route featuring significant elevation changes and dramatic rock formations. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e974800400/arguis-reservoir-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Arguis Reservoir Loop&lt;/a&gt;, known for its climbs and scenic views.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy most about cycling in Hoya de Huesca?
A: The komoot community highly rates touring cycling in Hoya de Huesca, with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 90 reviews. Cyclists often praise the region's diverse landscapes, from the striking Mallos to the serene reservoirs, and the opportunity to combine natural beauty with historical exploration.

Q: Can I find routes that combine natural beauty with cultural exploration?
A: Many routes in Hoya de Huesca are designed to showcase both its natural beauty and historical significance. You can pedal through areas offering views of Romanesque architecture and ancient Roman roads. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38182187" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alerre – Huesca loop from Huesca&lt;/a&gt; provides a good balance of rural landscapes and proximity to historical towns.

Q: Are there routes that explore the Pre-Pyrenean mountains?
A: Yes, the foothills of the Pyrenees offer dramatic scenery and challenging rides. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/6954569" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Plaza de Luis López Allué – Montearagón Castle loop from Lierta&lt;/a&gt; will take you through mountainous terrain, providing diverse environments and stunning vistas.

Q: Are there any routes that pass by reservoirs or offer water views?
A: Yes, the region features serene reservoirs that are popular cycling spots. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e979471478/sotonera-reservoir-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sotonera Reservoir Loop&lt;/a&gt; is an excellent choice for those seeking routes with water views and generally flatter terrain. The Arguis and Vadiello reservoirs also offer beautiful surroundings for cycling.
tips:
This town in the region of Hoya de Huesca in the community of Aragón appears mentioned for the first time in 1104, in a concord between the bishop of Huesca and the abbot of Montearagón. Its origin is Roman, a link that still survives in the tradition of the "vote", which unites Alerre with six other towns that surround Huesca and form the "Seven Places".

This historical community was united by the promise of a vote destined to ask for the holy protection of a saint, Lorenzo, son of two Roman subjects who lived in a Roman settlement near present-day Huesca, and who was martyred in Rome.

Alerre has an interesting costume and footwear museum, located in the keep, next to the town hall, and which collects more than 3,000 pieces from all over the world, donated by the Scottish diplomat of Spanish origin Tomás Moyano.
The Alberca de Cortés is a diversion of the Isuela river where you can find a natural space with a large number of nesting birds. It dates from the 13th century.
Path parallel to the Río Isuela that winds along the bed of the same river until reaching the capital of Huesca. This path, between waters, reaches the Fuentes de Marcelo, near the town of Chimillas.

Huesca is the capital of one of the three Aragonese provinces, Huesca. the city, with a very large old town, a set of well-known architectural elements appear: the museum, the cathedral or the monastery are the case. Also the Plaça de Toros or the Plaça de D. Luis López Allué.

waypoints.name: Path to the Fuentes de Marcelo
